"user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-42290", "cvss": {"score": 7.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-78", "name": "Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')"}], "description": "## Summary

`pbts` invoked JSDoc by building a shell command string from input file paths and executing it through `child_process.exec`. File paths containing shell metacharacters could therefore be interpreted by the shell instead of being passed to JSDoc as plain arguments.

## Impact

An attacker who can control file names or paths passed to `pbts` may be able to execute arbitrary shell commands with the privileges of the process running `pbts`.

This affects the protobufjs CLI tooling path. The protobufjs runtime APIs for encoding, decoding, parsing, and loading protobuf messages are not directly affected by this issue.

## Preconditions

- The application or user must invoke `pbts` on file paths influenced by an attacker.
- The attacker must be able to supply or create a path containing shell-significant characters.
- The vulnerable `pbts` version must execute the generated JSDoc command through a shell.

## Workarounds

Do not run affected versions of `pbts` on attacker-controlled file names or paths. If this cannot be avoided, sanitize or rename input files before invoking `pbts`, or run the CLI in an isolated environment with minimal privileges.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00132, "percentile": 0.03081}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-f84p-cvgm-xgjj", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-12T14:59:45Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-f84p-cvgm-xgjj", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-f84p-cvgm-xgjj"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-42290"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-13T16:16:47Z", "published_at": "2026-05-12T14:59:45Z", "references": ["https://github.com/protobufjs/protobuf.js/security/advisories/GHSA-f84p-cvgm-xgjj", "https://github.com/protobufjs/protobuf.js/releases/tag/protobufjs-cli-v1.2.1", "https://github.com/protobufjs/protobuf.js/releases/tag/protobufjs-cli-v2.0.2", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-42290", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-f84p-cvgm-xgjj"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/protobufjs/protobuf.js/security-advisories/GHSA-f84p-cvgm-xgjj", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/protobufjs/protobuf.js", "summary": "protobuf.js is Vulnerable to OS Command Injection in the CLI", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-14T20:31:53Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-f84p-cvgm-xgjj", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.2.1", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "protobufjs-cli"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 1.2.0"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.0.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "protobufjs-cli"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.0.0, <= 2.0.1"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: protobuf.js is Vulnerable to OS Command Injection in the CLI
